data: A funerary stele in the form of a fluted column supporting a rectangular block bearing the inscribed field (the block is 26cm high by 57 cm wide)
Four lines of Greek text, engraved in stucco and painted in red, across the full width of the rectangular block, with a vacat below.
Suggested to date to the later 3rd or earlier 2nd century BCE
Museo archeologico regionale Lilibeo Marsala - Baglio Anselmi
Comprehensive collection of Archaeological material discovered at the site of Lilybaeum and in the surrounding area
Association pour l’encouragement des études greques, « Bulletin épigraphique », Revue des études grecques, 1888, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/797735566
M.T. Manni Piraino, «Due Iscrizioni inedite di Marsala», Kokalos 9 (1963): 157–62
Lilibeo. Testimonianze archeologiche dal IV sec a.C. al V sec. d.C.
C.A. Di Stefano, Lilibeo. Testimonianze archeologiche dal IV sec a.C. al V sec. d.C. (Palermo, 1984)

Manni Piraino, 1963, Due Iscrizioni inedite di Marsala
Di Stefano, 1984, Lilibeo. Testimonianze archeologiche dal IV sec a.C. al V sec. d.C.

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Manni Piraino

Italian translation

Tengo fra le braccia, o passeggero, un eccellente uomo straniero, Poseidermos, abitante di Marsiglia e figlio caro di Pitagora, che la Moira violenta ha ucciso dopo averlo trascinato lungi dalla cara patria

Tengo fra le braccia, o passeggero, un eccellente uomo straniero, Poseidermos, abitante di Marsiglia e figlio caro di Pitagora, che la Moira violenta ha ucciso dopo averlo trascinato lungi dalla cara patria

A funerary stele in the form of a fluted column supporting a rectangular block bearing the inscribed field (the block is 26cm high by 57 cm wide)

stone column 75 37

Four lines of Greek text, engraved in stucco and painted in red, across the full width of the rectangular block, with a vacat below.

chiselled Rubrication, red coloured Line 1 Interlineation line 1 to 2 Sicilia Lilybaeum Marsala 37.80355, 12.4379 Suggested to date to the later 3rd or earlier 2nd century BCE Original discovery not recorded. On display. Metcalfe 2015.04.18
